steps.title: Put the equation in quadratic form
steps.content: Start with the given equation: $$x(13x+27)+c=5$$ Expanding and moving everything to one side gives $$13x^2+27x+(c-5)=0.$$

steps.title: Match the given radical
steps.content: For $13x^2+27x+(c-5)=0$, the quadratic formula has denominator $2(13)=26$, matching the given solution. The expression under the square root must be $261$.

steps.title: Confirm algebraically
steps.content: Set the discriminant equal to $261$: $$27^2-4(13)(c-5)=261$$ $$989-52c=261$$ $$c=14$$ The result matches.
